author: draganrakita
content:
Few things.
* `stateGasRefunded` , `cumulativeStateGasRefunded` doesn't make sense to add, as those are internal
* `stateGasCharged` not sure why it is `Charged` and not `Used`
* `regularGasUsed` can be derived from `stateGasCharged` as `gasUsed- stateGasCharged`
* `cumulativeRegularGasUsed` dont see benefit of adding this one as even old field `cumulativeGasUsed` is strange one.

author: draganrakita
content: Possible design on state fields in Receipt

author: qu0b
content:
> stateGasRefunded , cumulativeStateGasRefunded doesn't make sense to add, as those are internal

Isn't the point of a debug RPC endpoint to expose internal information? I see that one could argue that we should move this to the tracer, but thats even more implementation work I would assume.

> stateGasCharged not sure why it is Charged and not Used

I wanted to separate the terms to make it clear if gas was actually used vs refunded, maybe it makes sense to simplify this, but we need some distiction so that it is easy to consume.

> regularGasUsed can be derived from stateGasCharged as gasUsed- stateGasCharged

From a implementation perspective I agree with you, but from a API consumer perspective I do not, it should be easy for someone to consume and not need this extra context on how to derive each value. 

> cumulativeRegularGasUsed dont see benefit of adding this one as even old field cumulativeGasUsed is strange one.

fine with me to remove the cumulative values

author: qu0b
content: I've updated and simplified the PR a bit given your feedback

author: qu0b
content: I'm still on the fence with `regularGasUsed` I think we should keep it

author: draganrakita
content: `stateGasRefunded ` is really fully internal to evm execution, not even sure what should we expose here, and any value would not make sense. It refund stuff that happened in same tx. In comparison regular refund adds additional "free" gas to the execution and in whole make it cheaper.
